key_insight: InvDesMobility adds a reliability gate to closed-loop inverse materials design so only sufficiently validated first-principles results are fed back into model training.
engineering_relevance: The framework makes high-throughput materials discovery more trustworthy and auditable by separating structural learning from property-learning and admitting expensive DFT results only after evidence checks.
method: Multi-agent automated DFT generates and validates candidate 2D materials.
Evidence is stratified into relaxed-structure feedback for the generative model and retained mobility channels for the acquisition model.
Reliability-gated records are used to rank new candidates and prioritize validation.
evidence_signal: From 516 2DMatPedia-derived candidates, the workflow produced 280 QC-passed materials and 573 retained carrier-direction seed channels, then screened 2.4×10^6 structures and submitted 102 candidates for DFT validation.
watch_item: The framework’s effectiveness depends on the quality of the gating criteria and workflow assumptions, so overly strict or noisy reliability filters could bias what the closed loop learns.
paper_title: InvDesMobility: a reliability-gated first-principles feedback framework for closed-loop materials discovery
